senses.definition: used to describe a person who always manages to arrive or get things done at the agreed or proper time

examples.x: Every evening at 8 PM, as regular as clockwork, my dad sits in his armchair and reads the newspaper for exactly 45 minutes.
Matt came round each Friday, regular as clockwork.
My grandfather was never late for anything, he was regular as clockwork.
One thing I loved about living in Germany was that the trains were always regular as clockwork.

senses.definition: a person who is innocent, kind, and agreeable

examples.x: Despite facing challenges, she always maintains an attitude of sweetness and light, spreading positivity wherever she goes.
His speeches are filled with sweetness and light, inspiring his audience with uplifting and optimistic ideas.
The book club discussion was a true embodiment of sweetness and light, as everyone shared their thoughts respectfully and focused on the positive aspects of the novel.

senses.definition: to treat stupid or annoying people with kindness and patience

examples.x: He is known for his sharp intellect and quick wit, but he often struggles to suffer fools gladly in social situations.
As a professor, she had little tolerance for incompetence and would not suffer fools in her classroom.
The CEO was known for being direct and expecting high levels of competence from his team, unwilling to suffer fools in the workplace.
During the intense debate, the politician struggled to hide his annoyance and made it clear that he had no intention to suffer fools.
The renowned chef demanded excellence in his kitchen and would not suffer fools who couldn't keep up with the fast-paced environment.

word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌnz hˈɑːɹt ɪz ɪnðə ɹˈaɪt plˈeɪs

senses.definition: having good intentions, even if the results are not perfect

word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt hˈɜːt hˈɑːɹm ɐ flˈaɪ flˈiː

senses.definition: to refrain from doing anything that would harm or threaten the life of any person or animal

examples.x: Despite his intimidating appearance, he's actually really kind-hearted and wouldn't harm a flea.
He may have a tough exterior, but deep down, he has a gentle heart and wouldn't hurt a flea.

word_list.pronunciation: hˈaɪd wˈʌnz lˈaɪt ˌʌndɚɹ ɐ bˈʊʃəl

senses.definition: to keep one's talents or abilities hidden from others, particularly due to one's modesty

examples.x: Despite her intelligence and knowledge, she often hides her light under a bushel, rarely speaking up in meetings.
He has exceptional leadership skills, but he tends to hide his light under a bushel and doesn't take on leadership roles.
She is such a talented artist but always hides her light under a bushel, rarely showcasing her work to others.

senses.definition: a sympathetic person who tries to help those who are in trouble or in desperate need of help

examples.x: He volunteered his time every weekend, serving meals at the community kitchen like a true Good Samaritan.

senses.definition: someone who is genuinely a good person

examples.x: If he continues his generous and caring nature, he'll be remembered as a good egg by everyone.
In the past, he was considered a good egg among his peers, always standing up for what was right.
Tom is a real good egg; he never hesitates to lend a hand to those in need.

senses.definition: (of a person) not showing pretentious behavior

examples.x: John is a CEO, but he's incredibly down to earth and always makes time for his employees.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who is very friendly and sympathetic

examples.x: Despite facing challenges, he remained kind and supportive, proving that he's as sweet as a pie.
My neighbor is always willing to lend a hand and is as sweet as a pie.
She greeted everyone with a warm smile and was as sweet as pie throughout the entire event.
The customer service representative was incredibly helpful and sweet as pie in assisting me with my issue.
Whenever we gather for family dinners, Aunt Jane bakes delicious desserts and is as sweet as pie to everyone.

word_list.pronunciation: æz pjˈʊɹ æz ðə dɹˈɪvən snˈoʊ

senses.definition: used to describe someone who is completely innocent and does not have any moral flaws

examples.x: Despite the temptations in the entertainment industry, she has remained as pure as the driven snow.
Even after all these years, her heart remains pure as the driven snow, untouched by bitterness or resentment.
His actions always reflect his pure as the driven snow character; he never compromises his values.
The child's innocence and honesty were as pure as the driven snow.

senses.definition: used to describe a very well-behaved and obedient individual, especially a child

examples.x: A lot of teenagers rebel, but not me—I was always good as gold.
She's been as good as gold all morning.
The children were as good as gold, waiting patiently at the entrance.

senses.definition: used to describe an individual who behaves in a very kind and gentle way

examples.x: Despite his size, he is as gentle as a lamb when playing with children.
She has a calming presence and is as gentle as a lamb in handling conflicts.
The nurse was gentle as a lamb while caring for the elderly patients.

senses.definition: someone who is much nicer and friendlier than they first appear

examples.x: Although initially reserved, Mike revealed himself to be a diamond in the rough, surprising everyone with his unwavering support and generosity.
Anna's initial aloofness faded away, revealing her as a diamond in the rough with a heart full of compassion and empathy.
At first, Sarah seemed distant, but she proved to be a diamond in the rough with her genuine warmth and kindness.
Despite his gruff exterior, John turned out to be a diamond in the rough, always offering a helping hand to those in need.
Mark's rough demeanor masked a diamond in the rough, as he showed an unexpected friendliness and willingness to lend a listening ear.

word_list.pronunciation: mˈæn ɔːɹ wˈʊmən ɔːɹ pˈɜːsən ʌv fjˈuː wˈɜːdz

senses.definition: someone who avoids talking much and manages to convey their message in the most concise way possible

examples.x: John is a man of few words, preferring to only speak when he has something meaningful to say.
Many admire Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas' wife Ginni for being a woman of few words who avoids the spotlight and controversy.
The strong, silent type, Frank has always been a man of few words.
ur professor seems like a person of few words, his lectures are concise without any extraneous details
The CEO is a man of few words, always focused and to the point.

senses.definition: a very shy or modest individual who tries not to attract others' attention

examples.x: Despite her skills and knowledge, Emily's shy nature makes her a shrinking violet in job interviews.
During group discussions, Sarah is a shrinking violet.
John is such a shrinking violet at parties.
She’s no shrinking violet, making bold moves in the business world without hesitation.
The shrinking violet in our class surprised everyone with her performance in the talent show.

senses.definition: used to describe a person who is surprisingly friendly and kind

examples.x: Despite facing numerous challenges, Sarah remained as nice as pie throughout the entire process, always maintaining a positive attitude and offering encouragement to her teammates.
Even though the customer was angry and frustrated, the customer service representative remained as sweet as pie, patiently listening to their concerns and finding a solution to their problem.
I was nervous about meeting my new neighbors, but they turned out to be nice as pie.
Samantha is always as nice as pie.
The teacher was always sweet as pie, treating each student with respect and creating a welcoming and inclusive classroom environment.
